channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rubio says El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said late Monday that El Salvador's president has offered to accept deportees from the U.S. of any nationality, including violent American criminals now imprisoned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Climate change could wipe out $1.4 trillion in real estate value
item.description: "Rrising insurance costs and population movements are transforming the economic geography of the nation.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: A Russia-like crackdown has jailed dozens in Georgia, with human rights groups sounding the alarm
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Jailed journalist Mzia Amaghlobeli gets weaker every day as her hunger strike has reached three weeks in Rustavi, a town near the Georgian capital of Tbilisi, her lawyer says.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump’s foreign aid freeze could prove to be a boon for the world’s authoritarian strongmen
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — When President Donald Trump froze foreign assistance for 90 days, he argued that such a drastic step was needed to eliminate waste and block what he derides as “woke” spending that doesn’t align with American interests.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trayon White channels Marion Barry as he fights corruption charges and expulsion from the DC Council
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Trayon White came up in local politics as a direct protege of iconic and infamous former Mayor Marion Barry. Now the D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hero 'band dads' take down elderly active shooter at Texas high school
item.description: An elderly man who opened fire at a high school band competition in Texas was apprehended by four tactically trained dads who deescalated the situation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump will use drones if cartels don't withdraw
item.description: "If they don't recede, you're gonna see dead bodies all over the place and that's coming soon," O'Reilly says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: What is USAID? Explaining the US foreign aid agency and why Trump and Musk want to end it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dozens of senior officials put on leave. Thousands of contractors laid off. A freeze put on billions of dollars in humanitarian assistance to other countries. Over the last two weeks, President Donald Trump's administration has made significant changes to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration opens antisemitism inquiries at 5 colleges including Columbia and Berkeley
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is opening new investigations into allegations of antisemitism at five U.S. universities including Columbia and the University of California, Berkeley, the Education Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary deportation protection for 350,000 Venezuelans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is ending protections that shielded roughly 350,000 Venezuelans from deportation, leaving them with two months before they lose their right to work in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Senate confirms fracking CEO Chris Wright to lead Energy Department
item.description: The Senate on Monday confirmed Chris Wright, the CEO of fracking company Liberty Energy, to lead the Energy Department. The vote was 59-38. Democrat Sens. Michael Bennet (Colo.), Ruben Gallego (Ariz.), Maggie Hassan (N.H.), Martin Heinrich (N.M.), John Hickenlooper (Colo.), Ben Ray Luján (N.M.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Duffy tells DOT to prioritize areas with high birth rates
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy in a memo told the Department of Transportation (DOT) to prioritize communities that have higher birth and marriage rates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Judge orders acquittal of ex-prosecutor on 1 of 2 counts in misconduct trial over Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — A judge on Monday ordered the acquittal of a former Georgia prosecutor on one of the two misconduct charges against her, ruling that prosecutors at her trial failed to present any evidence the she obstructed a police investigation of the 2020 killing of Ahmaud .Arbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pacific Coast Highway reopens nearly a month after devastating Southern California wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Pacific Coast Highway reopened Monday, nearly a month after it was closed when flames from the deadly Palisades Fire ripped through hillside Los Angeles neighborhoods, destroying properties all the way down to the beach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Thune on Trump tariffs: 'We’ll see how long' they last
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) told reporters Monday he’s taking a wait-and-see approach to President Trump’s order to place 25-percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico and a 10-percent tariff on China, which sent stock markets dropping.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York shields abortion pill prescribers after a doctor was indicted in Louisiana
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul on Monday signed a bill to shield the identities of doctors who prescribe abortion medications, days after a physician in the state was charged with prescribing abortion pills to a pregnant minor in Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico for a month
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum and U.S. President Donald Trump have agreed to put planned tariffs on hold for a month. Trump’s tariffs against Canada and China are still slated to go into effect on Tuesday. Staffers of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Number injured following Philadelphia plane crash increases to 24, some remain hospitalized
item.description: Philadelphia's mayor and U.S. Department of Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy spoke at a news conference Monday alongside investigators of the deadly medical jet crash investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missouri restaurant says ICE arrested some employees at work
item.description: "I grew up, like, just right down the road from here. I always come to eat maybe once or twice a week at least," customer Autmn Emery said.
item.dc:creator: Chad Mira, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Even those who have not worked directly in DEI roles but went to the training have been put on administrative leave.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Recall for broccoli sold at Walmart in 20 states upgraded to highest risk level: FDA
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has upgraded its classification of a broccoli recall initially reported in late December, labeling it as “Class I” — the agency’s most serious recall category.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Trump announces pause on Mexico tariffs after striking deal
item.description: Trump revealed he negotiated assistance from Mexico to strengthen the United States' southern border.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis to pay woman who says Chauvin knelt on her back. Here's how much
item.description: The city of Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to a woman who alleged that former policeman Derek Chauvin pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just months before he used the same maneuver on George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Lady Gaga defends transgender community during Grammys speech
item.description: "The trans community deserves to be lifted up," Lady Gaga, who was born Stefani Germanotta, said to applause.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Journaling is hard, but this smart new app makes it easier
item.description: Keeping a journal isn't easy, but the benefits are clear. "The need we have to try to make sense or our experience is deep," said David Coates, a psychotherapist and coach.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Salvage crews recover engine, large portion of jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: Salvage crews have recovered an engine and large pieces of fuselage and are working to retrieve a wing from wreckage of last week’s midair collision near Washington’s Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: David R. Martin, Michael R. Sisak And Claudia Lauer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Arkansas nurse charged with using dead patient's name in attempt to purchase hundreds of oxycodone pills
item.description: A nurse in Arkansas is facing felony charges after investigators say she used a dead man's name when attempting to purchase oxycodone pills at pharmacies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chappell Roan wins best new artist Grammy Award
item.description: Chappell Roan nudged aside Sabrina Carpenter to win the Grammy for best new artist Sunday, capping a remarkably explosive rise to fame for an artist dropped by her last record label.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chase Elliott wins Clash at Bowman Gray and sets sights on following it with Daytona 500 victory
item.description: NASCAR returned to its roots with a fight-free preseason exhibition at Bowman Gray Stadium and a popular victory with Chase Elliott winning The Clash on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Coast Guard cutter intercepts boat with 16 Mexicans off coast of California
item.description: The Coast Guard intercepted a boat carrying 16 illegal immigrants from Mexico about 15 miles off the coast of southern California on Sunday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USAID security leaders on leave after trying to keep Musk's DOGE from classified info: officials
item.description: The two USAID security officials believed themselves legally obligated to deny access to Musk's DOGE crew since it lacked high-enough security clearance to access the information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Remains of North Carolina mom missing since 2023 found by dive team: deputies
item.description: Dana Mustian never showed up at her daughter’s 6th birthday party in December 2023. Her remains have since been located by a dive team.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Iowa honors Caitlin Clark by retiring her number and hanging it in the rafters
item.description: Clark returned to Iowa’s Carver-Hawkeye Arena on Sunday to have her number 22 retired in a ceremony after the Hawkeyes’ 76-67 win over No. 4 USC, one season after she completed her historic college career.
item.dc:creator: JOHN BOHNENKAMP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hershey isn't sure why Kisses are called 'Kisses'
item.description: Why isn't it called a Hershey’s Smooch or a Hershey’s Cone? Even the people at Hershey aren’t exactly sure.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC
